key_themes: Clinical progress and promising early data for pre|CISION PDCs (AVA6000/AVA6103)
Oversubscribed £10m equity raise extending runway to early 2027 with full pipeline ownership
Buy analyst consensus, management strengthening, and improving retail/X sentiment
sources_checked: Avacta company Q1 2025 & Q1 2026 business updates
Peel Hunt/MarketBeat analyst ratings and price targets
Recent X posts (keyword and semantic search)
summary: Avacta is demonstrating clear momentum in its tumor-targeted oncology platform with first-patient dosing of AVA6103 in Phase 1 (March 2026), favorable preclinical comparisons to Enhertu, lifted dose limits and encouraging PFS for AVA6000 that nearly doubles certain benchmarks, plus an oversubscribed £10m raise in late March 2026 that removes near-term funding pressure.[[1]](https://avacta.com/q1-2026-business-update/)[[2]](https://avacta.com/avacta-provides-business-update-for-the-first-quarter-of-2025-outlining-progress-against-strategic-objectives/) Analysts largely rate the stock Buy with 20-50%+ upside from ~65-77p levels, new experienced CFO/NEDs are in place, and X chatter has turned strongly bullish on the turnaround while noting reduced overhang from the legacy Heights convertible bond (amended to defer payments and favor cash settlement where possible).[[3]](https://www.marketbeat.com/stocks/LON/AVCT/forecast/)[[4]](https://avacta.com/amendments-to-the-convertible-bond-and-equity-fundraise-of-3-25-million/) The biggest risk remains binary 2026 clinical readouts and potential further dilution if additional capital is required beyond the current runway.

recent_posts.highlight: Compares AVCT potential to Merck's interest in Revolution Medicines ($32B pre-revenue); highlights pre|CISION utility
Positive signals and promo since first patient dosing for 6103; expects US investor interest and major re-rating vs. current suppressed valuation
Progressed from single-asset story to broader platform; clinical risk reduced, balance sheet strengthened by £10m raise
We could be very close to a pivotal period in Avacta history (with chart)
